page: Amazon Web Services m8azn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R05 processor architecture running at a high clock speed of 5.0 GHz. The processor includes an extensive instruction set, featuring AVX-512 vector extensions such as AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. While the server does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. This high network throughput, combined with EBS optimization, ensures rapid data transfer rates for remote storage volumes.

Benchmark results place the m8azn.12xlarge in the top tier (top 10%) across multiple performance dimensions. It demonstrates exceptional single-core and multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests, alongside top-tier memory bandwidth, reaching 529.59 GB/sec for large-block read operations. Database operations, Redis SET performance at 176.14 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 113.64 GB/sec also rank in the top 10%. Additionally, the server exhibits strong capabilities in LLM inference, processing 324.67 tokens per second on medium 7B models.

Given its hardware profile, the m8azn.12xlarge offers high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high-frequency single-thread performance and substantial memory bandwidth without the overhead of local storage. It is highly suited for high-throughput web serving, database management, software compilation, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an AMD EPYC 9R05 processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores running at 5.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz. While it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Benchmark data places this instance in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. It demonstrates exceptional throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving. This server is highly suited for high-performance web serving, database workloads, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.12xlarge is a general-purpose server with 48 dedicated 5.0 GHz AMD EPYC cores, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier compute and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.12xlarge features 48 AMD EPYC cores at 5.0 GHz, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency AMD EPYC server featuring dedicated cores, DDR5 memory, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for demanding general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 physical cores running at 5.0 GHz.
192.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz.
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with EBS optimization.
Top-tier CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance.
Optimized for high-throughput web serving, database operations, and LLM inference.
